To determine the equilibrium population size $ P_e $ where the population remains constant over time we set the derivative $ \frac{dP}{dt} $ equal to zero. This gives the equation:

$$ 0 = rP\left(1 - \frac{P}{K}\right) - h(t) $$

Substituting the given values $ r = 0.1 $, $ K = 1000 $, and $ h(t) = 10 + 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) $ we obtain:

$$ 0 = 0.1P\left(1 - \frac{P}{1000}\right) - 10 - 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) $$

To find the equilibrium population $ P_e $ we consider the average value of the harvesting function over a full period. The function $ h(t) = 10 + 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) $ has a period of 12 years since the sine function repeats every $ 2\pi $ and $ \frac{\pi}{6}t = 2\pi $ when $ t = 12 $. The average value of $ \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) $ over a full period is zero. Therefore the average harvesting rate is $ 10 $.

Substituting the average harvesting rate into the equation gives:

$$ 0 = 0.1P\left(1 - \frac{P}{1000}\right) - 10 $$

Simplifying this equation:

$$ 0.1P\left(1 - \frac{P}{1000}\right) = 10 $$

$$ 0.1P - \frac{0.1P^2}{1000} = 10 $$

$$ 0.1P - 0.0001P^2 = 10 $$

$$ 0.0001P^2 - 0.1P + 10 = 0 $$

Multiplying through by 10000 to eliminate decimals:

$$ P^2 - 1000P + 100000 = 0 $$
